title: FOOD PROGRAMS - 211info
text: SNOWCAP COMMUNITY CHARITIES FOOD PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides fresh food boxes including meat, vegetables, dairy items, bread, baby food, and formula. Pet food is regularly available in the pantry. Limited monthly Senior Home Deliveries provided. People are welcome to receive food boxes two times per month. Also provides TriMet bus fares. The Community Garden is available to low-income families to raise their own vegetables. Water, greenhouse space, and gardening classes are provided. Gardeners give back by giving 10% of their harvest to the pantry. 17805 SE Stark Street Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Food pantry and community garden: Low-income households Formula: Age 2 and under Monthly Senior Home Deliveries: Homebound seniors age 62 and older Hours: Monday/Wednesday 10am-2pm and 6pm-8pm, Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 10am-2pm Email (503) 674-8785 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (503) 674-8785 Main phone Main phone (503) 674-8785 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Walk-up window for food distribution. First time clients need to fill out application before receiving services. Visit website for intake forms for SnowCap application or TriMet fare requests. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Multnomah County TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Behind the Rockwood Center Services: Bus Fare Community Gardening Food Pantries Formula/Baby Food Pet Food If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. SNOWCAP COMMUNITY CHARITIES
